creator: erdgeist@erdgeist.org
text:
On 01.05.13 11:07, Stefan Walter wrote:

>> We would like to be able to name the jail directory, the jail name and
>> the jails host name consistently.
>> (Our naming convention defines using a '-' character to distinguish network interface names on a server)eg.
>>
>> export jail_server-j1_hostname="server-j1.domain.local"
>>
>> export jail_server-j1_rootdir="/jails/server-j1"

This is not a limitation of ezjail. It is a limitation of the shell and
thus the jail sub-system.

Quoting the sh man page:

"Variables set by the user must have a name consisting solely of
alphabetics, numerics, and underscores.  The first letter of a variable
name must not be numeric."

IOW: The lines you pasted above do not even work on the command line.

ezjail allows you to keep your naming scheme, using the hostname for you
as a handle and derives the jailname from the hostname, with the
aforementioned limitations.

With the advent of the new jail(8) command, allowing to specify jail
configurations in a config file rather than in shell variables, it has
become possible to set jailnames that contain more characters, including
the '-', but not '.'

Regards,

  erdgeist
